vocab.meaning: giành quyền kiểm soát/quyết định mọi việc
vocab.example: I'm a bit of a control freak, so I prefer to be the driver and call the shots regarding which route we take.

vocab.meaning: đóng vai trò phụ/nhường quyền kiểm soát cho người khác
vocab.example: After a stressful week at work, I’m more than happy to take a back seat and let my husband do all the driving.

vocab.meaning: ở vị trí kiểm soát/chủ động
vocab.example: I feel much more secure when I'm in the driving seat, as I can react instantly to any hazards on the road.
Being a passenger gives me a rare window of time to just sit back, listen to music, and recharge my batteries.

vocab.meaning: cảnh giác cao độ/quan sát kỹ lưỡng
vocab.example: As a driver, you have to keep your eyes peeled for erratic motorbikes, which can be quite exhausting in this city.
I definitely prefer the passenger side on long haul trips; it’s the perfect excuse to put my feet up and enjoy the ride.

vocab.meaning: đi từ nơi này đến nơi khác (nhấn mạnh sự tiện lợi)
vocab.example: I don't really care about the thrill of driving; for me, a car is just a tool to get from A to B as a passenger.

vocab.meaning: làm ai đó phát điên/bực mình
vocab.example: I avoid being a passenger for too long because slow drivers really drive me up the wall.

vocab.meaning: ngắm nhìn thế giới trôi qua (thường là qua cửa sổ)
vocab.example: There is something poetic about sitting in the back and watching the world go by through a rain-streaked window.

vocab.meaning: phó mặc cho/lệ thuộc hoàn toàn vào
vocab.example: The downside of being a passenger is that you are at the mercy of the driver’s music taste and air-con settings.
